# v2.2.0 — Agentic Flow Improvements

**Date:** 2025-07-15
**Branch:** `feature-2-v2.2.0`
**Scope:** Agent instructions, templates, quality tooling, workflow architecture
**Excluded:** Agent hooks (separate increment), Coder model change (keeping GPT-5.3-Codex)

---

## Change Log

### Priority 1 — Quick Wins

| Fix | File | Change |
|-----|------|--------|
| 1.1 | `java-architect.agent.md` | Verified: model already set to single value `Claude Sonnet 4.6 (copilot)` — no edit needed |
| 1.2 | `java-coder.agent.md` | Added curl smoke-test step in Verification section (step 5) before GitLab endpoint re-verification |
| 1.3 | `java-coder.agent.md` | Added per-batch handoff mandate: "Return structured delivery summary after EVERY batch" |
| 1.8 | `java-coder.agent.md` | Added no-unplanned-files constraint: "Do not create any files not defined in the plan slices" |
| 1.4 | `team-lead.agent.md` | Added curl smoke-check gate in Coder Invocation Strategy before Reviewer Phase 2 |
| 1.5 | `team-lead.agent.md` | Clarified red card counter tracks only coder-caused false positives, not plan/process issues |
| 1.6 | `test-instructions.instructions.md` | Added Mockito matcher rule (all args must use matchers), string constant dedup rule |
| 1.7 | `code-guidance.md` | Strengthened `@RequiredArgsConstructor` from "preferred" to "required" with explicit constructor ban |

### Priority 2 — Context Budget

| Fix | File | Change |
|-----|------|--------|
| 2.1a | `java-architect.agent.md` | Added plan line cap: "MUST NOT exceed 200 lines excluding JSON payload examples" |
| 2.1b | `implementation-plan-template.md` | Removed Ownership, Risks/Notes, Linked Handoff sections; merged 4 payload example subsections into 1 |
| 2.2 | `review-report-template.json` | Compacted all phase1Checks and phase2Checks field names (e.g. `implementationMatchesPlanOrApprovedDeviations` → `implMatchesPlan`) |

### Priority 3 — Quality Tooling

| Fix | File | Change |
|-----|------|--------|
| 3.1 | `spotbugs-exclude.xml` | Reviewed: intentionally empty, no false positives to narrow — no edit needed |
| 3.2 | `checkstyle.xml` | Added `MultipleStringLiterals` rule with `allowedDuplicates=2` |
| 3.3 | `test-instructions.instructions.md` | Added: "Each plan slice must include at least one edge-case or boundary-condition test" |

### Priority 5 — Workflow Architecture (partial)

| Fix | File | Change |
|-----|------|--------|
| 5.1 | `scripts/smoke-test.sh` | NEW: Reusable smoke-test script (health, issues API default + filtered), exit 1 on failure |
| 5.3 | `implementation-report-template.json` | Added `smokeCheckEvidence` field (scriptUsed, exitCode, passCount, failCount, summary) |
| 5.3 | `implementation-signoff-template.json` | Added `smokeCheckEvidence` field (same structure) |

### Deferred

| Item | Reason |
|------|--------|
| Priority 4 — Agent hooks | Separate increment per user decision |
| Fix 5.2 — Coder model upgrade | User wants to give GPT-5.3-Codex another chance |

---

## Files Changed

### Agent Definitions

- `.github/agents/java-coder.agent.md` — curl step, no-unplanned-files, per-batch handoff
- `.github/agents/team-lead.agent.md` — curl gate before P2, red card counter clarification

### Instructions
- `.github/instructions/test-instructions.instructions.md` — Mockito matchers, string constants, edge-case rule

### Guidance
- `documentation/code-guidance.md` — `@RequiredArgsConstructor` mandate

### Templates
- `artifacts/templates/implementation-plan-template.md` — slimmed (Ownership, Risks, Linked Handoff removed; payloads merged)
- `artifacts/templates/review-report-template.json` — compacted field names
- `artifacts/templates/implementation-report-template.json` — smokeCheckEvidence field
- `artifacts/templates/implementation-signoff-template.json` — smokeCheckEvidence field

### Quality Config
- `flow-orchestrator/config/quality/checkstyle.xml` — MultipleStringLiterals rule

### Scripts
- `scripts/smoke-test.sh` — NEW reusable smoke-test script

## Verification

- [ ] `scripts/quality-check.sh` passes (Checkstyle rule must not flag existing code or be validated against current codebase)
- [ ] All edited files syntactically valid
- [ ] `scripts/smoke-test.sh` is executable and runs cleanly against a started app

## Flow Setup Diagram

This diagram shows the intended `v2.2.0` flow for the `create-issue-api` run based on the agent definitions, the orchestration prompt, and the shared artifact gates.

### Planning Phase Diagram

```
┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                           INTENDED FLOW                                  │
│                                                                          │
│   1:TL ──────► 2:PM ──────► 3:TL ──────► 4:ARCH ───────────► 5:TL    │
│    chkpt+req    story.md      story.md     plan.md              story+plan│
│                                                                          │
│   5:TL ────────────────────────────────► 6:REV1 ──────────────► 7:TL  │
│    story + plan + chkpt                   review.json (Phase 1)          │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

### Implementation Phase Diagram

```
┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                                                                          │
│   7:TL ────────────────────────────────► 8:CODER ────────────► 9:TL   │
│    approved slices (max 2/batch)           report.json + smoke-test      │
│                                                                          │
│   9:TL ────────────────────────────────► 10:REV2 ────────────► 11:TL  │
│    phase2-brief + impl evidence            review.json          sign-off │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

### Flow Step Clarification

- **1:TL** — Requirement lock, writes `create-issue-api-checkpoint.json`; hands off `chkpt + locked request` to PM
- **2:PM** — Produces `artifacts/user-stories/create-issue-api.story.md`; story must preserve locked scope and acceptance criteria
- **3:TL** — Story gate; hands off `story.md` to Java Architect
- **4:ARCH** — Produces `artifacts/implementation-plans/create-issue-api.plan.md`; plan must include slices, payloads, validation boundaries, logging, and tests
- **5:TL** — Plan gate; hands off `story + plan + chkpt` to Reviewer Phase 1
- **6:REV1** — Produces `artifacts/review-reports/create-issue-api.review.json`; no `FAIL` or `BLOCKED` items allowed
- **7:TL** — Phase 1 gate; hands off approved slices (max 2/batch) to Java Coder
- **8:CODER** — Produces `artifacts/implementation-reports/create-issue-api.report.json` + `create-issue-api-verification.log`, updates `scripts/smoke-test.sh` if API changed; evidence must be complete and recheckable
- **9:TL** — Coder gate, independent verification; hands off `phase2-brief.md + impl evidence` to Reviewer Phase 2
- **10:REV2** — Produces `artifacts/review-reports/create-issue-api.review.json`; no unresolved verification gaps
- **11:TL** — Final audit gate; produces `artifacts/implementation-signoffs/create-issue-api.signoff.json`

            throw handleTransportFailure(exception.getClass().getSimpleName());
        }

## Post-Run Analysis — v2.2.0 System Assessment

**Analyzed by:** GitHub Copilot (Claude Opus 4.6)
**Analysis date:** 2026-04-02
**Scope:** Full agent orchestration system v2.2.0 run, all generated code, all artifacts, and comparison with v2.1.0

---

### Evolution Scorecard

| Version | Key Change | Run Time | Context Used | Coder Loops | Code Quality |
|---------|-----------|----------|--------------|-------------|--------------|
| v1.0.1 | Baseline | ~2h | ~70% (rot killed it) | N/A | Manual fixes needed |
| v2.0.0 | Checkpoint JSON, circuit breaker | 2h+ | ~50% (Reviewer corrupted) | 4+ | Working, no fixes |
| v2.1.0 | JSON artifacts, local quality toolchain | ~1h | ~26% at handoff | ~2 | Stable, readable |
| v2.2.0 | Curl smoke, per-batch handoff, plan cap | 1h40m | ~40% | 3+ (coder degraded) | Auto-generated feel |

**Verdict:** v2.2.0 was a regression in run time and code quality despite workflow improvements. The root cause is clear: **the Coder agent is the system bottleneck**, and adding more process around it (smoke tests, handoff mandates, no-unplanned-files) made things *worse* because the Coder now has more instructions to follow and more reasons to stall.

### Actual Flow Execution Diagram

This diagram shows how the flow actually behaved in the `v2.2.0` run based on the user notes and the final on-disk artifacts.

#### Planning Phase - Actual

```
┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                          ACTUAL FLOW — v2.2.0                            │
│                                                                          │
│   1:TL ─── 2:PM×1 ─✗─ 2:PM×2 ─✓─ 3:TL ─── 4:ARCH ─── 5:TL           │
│   chkpt    req mismatch  story.md    story     plan.md     story+plan    │
│                                                                          │
│   5:TL ────────────────────────────────── 6:REV1 ──────────── 7:TL     │
│    story + plan + chkpt                    review.json (PASS)            │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

#### Implementation Phase - Actual

```
┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                                                                              │
│  B1  8:CODER×1 ─✗─ 8:CODER×2 ─✓─ 9:TL                                    │
│       no handoff    report.json                                              │
│                                                                              │
│  B2  8:CODER×1 ─✗─ 8:CODER×2 ─✗─ 8:CODER×3 ─✗─ 8:CODER×4 ─✓─ 9:TL     │
│       checkstyle    no summary     smoke gap       health mis.  report.json  │
│                                                                              │
│      9:TL ──── 10:REV2×1 ─✗─ 10:REV2×2 ─✓─ 11:TL ──── SIGN-OFF           │
│      brief     art. outdated    review.json                                  │
│                                                                              │
│  CTX  ████  ██  ████   ██   ████████████████████████████   ██   ▓▓▓        │
│                                        ↑                                     │
│                                   ctx pressure (B2: 4 coder loops)           │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

#### Actual Flow Deviations

- **Planning deviations:** PM×1 rejected — story did not hold the locked requirement correctly; PM×2 accepted. Architect and Phase 1 Reviewer required no reruns.
- **Coder loops:** B1 rejected once (missing handoff despite green checks); B2 rejected 3 times (Checkstyle findings, missing summary, smoke evidence gap, health endpoint mismatch). 4 total coder rejections across both batches.
- **Reviewer reruns:** REV2 reported PASS in chat but on-disk artifact was not updated; TL issued red card; REV2 rerun produced clean on-disk artifact.
- **Biggest single cost or quality impact:** B2's 4-rejection loop — consumed the majority of run time and context budget, and exposed the Coder's continued difficulty with handoff completeness and verification evidence.

---

### Strengths (5)

1. **Smoke test script works.** `scripts/smoke-test.sh` is a real artifact — reusable, scriptable, and agents eventually use it. The concept is validated.
2. **Red card counter clarification helped.** TL correctly distinguished coder false positives from process issues. No false escalations to Architect this run.
3. **Per-batch handoff mandate was partially effective.** Coder eventually returned structured summaries (after 1 rejection). The mandate makes failures visible faster.
4. **Plan line cap is good intent.** The 200-line directive exists in the Architect agent. Whether the Architect respects it depends on the next run.
5. **Checkstyle `MultipleStringLiterals` rule catches real issues.** String literal duplication was a recurring code quality complaint — now automated.

---

### Gaps (10)

#### Gap 1 — Coder is the bottleneck (recurring since v1.0.1)

**Evidence:** 50+ mins of 1h40m total was Coder time. Coder degraded to random terminal commands, failed to return handoffs, fought with shell scripting, and required multiple TL rejections. This is the 4th consecutive run where Coder is the weakest agent.

**Root cause analysis:**
- Coder agent prompt is now ~150 lines of constraints + verification requirements. The model (GPT-5.3-Codex) struggles to hold all constraints simultaneously.
- Coder is asked to: implement code, write tests, run verify-quick, run quality-check, update smoke-test.sh, start the app, run curl, update the verification log, write a structured handoff summary, checkpoint progress, not create extra files, record max 5 lines per command. That is 12+ responsibilities in a single agent invocation.
- Terminal command construction is a persistent failure mode. Coder builds multi-line bash scripts to capture output, parse results, and write logs — and these scripts repeatedly fail.

**Verdict:** More instructions will not fix the Coder. The problem is cognitive overload.

#### Gap 2 — Code quality regressed ("auto-generated feel")

**Evidence from run notes:**
- "Naming of the classes is very poor — just a lot of random names without common logic"
- "Chaos in project structure and extra DTOs that can be avoided"
- "Sometimes mapping doesn't have any sense — models are overcomplicating simple things"
- Duplicate `catch` blocks (`RestClientException` and `RuntimeException` with identical handling)
- `CreateIssueCommand` uses defensive null→List.of() pattern that obscures intent
- Response DTOs are 1:1 mirrors of domain models (unnecessary layer)

**Root cause:** No explicit naming conventions or package structure rules in `code-guidance.md`. The Architect and Coder invent structure ad-hoc each run. Without a reference package layout, each feature produces a different naming style.

#### Gap 3 — Reviewer adds minimal value

**Evidence from run notes:** "No added value here almost. Reviewer is expected to catch observations like I put below." Reviewer re-ran scripts (verify-quick, quality-check, smoke-test) but performed no meaningful code review. Did not catch naming chaos, duplicate catch blocks, unnecessary DTOs, or the `Command` naming question.

**Root cause:** Reviewer instructions focus on checklist execution (run scripts → check pass/fail → report). There is no instruction to review code *quality*, *naming*, *design decisions*, or *over-engineering*. The Reviewer is a terminal runner, not a code reviewer.

#### Gap 4 — Excessive TL↔Coder ping-pong

**Evidence:** TL rejected Coder 3+ times. Each rejection costs: TL reads artifacts → runs `mvn test` → writes rejection → invokes Coder again → Coder re-reads context → fixes → re-verifies → re-submits. At v2.2.0 scale (2 features, 4 slices), this consumed ~30 mins and significant premium request budget.

**Root cause:** TL verification dupplication. TL independently runs `mvn test` after Coder claims green — but then Reviewer *also* runs `mvn test`. Three agents running the same command is waste, not safety.

#### Gap 5 — Coder creates terminal "mega-commands"

**Evidence (directly from run notes):**
```bash
LOG="artifacts/implementation-reports/create-issue-api-verification.log" && mkdir -p artifacts/implementation-reports && echo "=== Slice 1: rename-fetch-endpoint | $(date -u) ===" >> "$LOG" && set +e && output=$(scripts/verify-quick.sh 2>&1) && exit_code=$? && ...
```

These 15-line bash scripts fail frequently and waste tokens on retry.

**Root cause:** Coder is instructed to "record ONLY: the command, exit code, test count, and failure summary" which it interprets as "I need to parse Maven output into structured data with bash". The instruction tries to limit output but causes the opposite — more complex terminal commands that fail more often.

#### Gap 6 — No package structure reference

The codebase now has 30+ Java files across 4 layers with no documented package layout. The Architect invents package structure per feature, and the Coder creates files wherever the plan says. Result: `GetIssuesPort` vs `CreateIssuePort` (plural vs singular), `GitLabIssuesAdapter` vs `GitLabCreateIssueAdapter`, 4 separate mapper classes in `orchestration/issues/api/` for what could be 1-2.

#### Gap 7 — Model proliferation (over-mapping)

Current model count for the "issues" feature alone: `Issue`, `CreatedIssue`, `IssuePage`, `IssueState`, `IssueQuery`, `CreateIssueCommand`, `IssuesRequest`, `PaginationRequest`, `IssueFiltersRequest`, `IssuesResponse`, `IssueResponseItem`, `CreateIssueRequest`, `CreateIssueResponse`, `GitLabIssueResponse` (with 3 nested records), `GitLabCreateIssueRequest`. That is **18 model classes** for 2 API endpoints. 5-6 of these are 1:1 mirrors.

#### Gap 8 — `Command` naming pattern is unnecessary

`CreateIssueCommand` is a simple data carrier (title, description, labels). It does not represent a CQRS command, is not dispatched to a command bus, carries no metadata (correlation ID, timestamp, actor). The `Command` suffix adds enterprise ceremony without enterprise benefit. A simple `CreateIssueRequest` at the orchestration layer (distinct from the API `CreateIssueRequest`) or just passing parameters would be clearer.

#### Gap 9 — Health endpoint mismatch wasted time

smoke-test.sh checks `/actuator/health` but the app didn't have `spring-boot-starter-actuator`. This was identified in v2.1.0 refactor notes but not addressed. TL sent Coder back to fix it — wasted time on infrastructure, not features.

#### Gap 10 — No formatting tool

Indentation issues spotted manually. Java has standardized formatting (google-java-format, spring-javaformat-maven-plugin) that can be added to the build pipeline. This is free quality.

---

### Context Budget Analysis (v2.2.0)

| Stage | Context Used | Delta |
|-------|-------------|-------|
| After PM + Architect | ~12% | — |
| After Coder batch 1 (slices 1-2) | ~20% | +8% |
| After Coder batch 2 (slices 3-4) | ~29% | +9% |
| Coder rejections + retries | ~33% | +4% (waste) |
| After Reviewer Phase 2 | ~37% | +4% |
| Final TL signoff | ~40% | +3% |

Coder accounted for ~17% of total context (batches + retries). TL↔Coder ping-pong added ~4% pure waste.

---

### Improvement Recommendations — v2.3.0

#### R1 — Simplify Coder responsibilities (HIGH IMPACT)

Split verification out of Coder. Coder should: (1) implement code, (2) write tests, (3) run `scripts/verify-quick.sh`, (4) return changed files list. That is 4 responsibilities, not 12+.

Move to TL: smoke-test execution, verification log formatting, implementation report updates. TL already runs these checks — let it own the evidence writing too.

Remove from Coder: startng the app, running curl, updating smoke-test.sh, writing verification logs, parsing terminal output into structured data.

#### R2 — Add code review to Reviewer (HIGH IMPACT)

Add explicit Reviewer Phase 2 checks for:
- Naming consistency (ports, adapters, services, mappers use consistent vocabulary)
- Model count justification (flag when response DTO is a 1:1 mirror of domain model)
- Duplicate code patterns (identical catch blocks, repeated null-check patterns)
- Over-engineering signals (unnecessary layers, wrappers, or abstractions)

#### R3 — Create package structure reference (HIGH IMPACT)

Document the canonical package layout in `code-guidance.md`. The Architect and Coder must follow it, not invent ad-hoc. See **Refactoring Plan** below for the proposed structure.

#### R4 — Add Java format plugin to the build (MEDIUM IMPACT)

Add `spring-javaformat-maven-plugin` or `fmt-maven-plugin` (google-java-format) to `pom.xml`. Run in `validate` phase. Eliminates all formatting debates and indentation issues.

#### R5 — Remove unnecessary handoff ceremony (MEDIUM IMPACT)

Consider: TL runs `mvn test` → Coder runs `verify-quick.sh` → Reviewer runs `quality-check.sh`. That is 3 overlapping verification runs. Instead: Coder runs `verify-quick.sh` (compile + test). TL skips independent `mvn test` if Coder's verify-quick exit code is 0 and TL can read the terminal output. Reviewer runs `quality-check.sh` (full gate) as the single quality checkpoint.

#### R6 — Simplify Coder evidence gathering (MEDIUM IMPACT)

Replace the "record max 5 lines" instruction with: "After running a verification script, report: `PASS` or `FAIL`. If FAIL, paste the last 10 lines of output. Do not parse Maven output with bash." This removes the incentive to build mega-commands.

#### R7 — Resolve health endpoint decision once (LOW IMPACT)

Either: add `spring-boot-starter-actuator` to `pom.xml` (2 lines), or: remove `/actuator/health` check from `smoke-test.sh` and replace with a simple `GET /api/issues/search` as a health probe. Decide once, document in `code-guidance.md`.

#### R8 — Consider single-port-per-feature pattern (LOW IMPACT)

Instead of `GetIssuesPort` + `CreateIssuePort` → single `IssuesPort` with `getIssues()` and `createIssue()`. Reduces interface count, aligns with the adapter (which is already one class per GitLab resource). Evaluate at the next architecture decision.

---

### Persistent Issues Tracker

| Issue | v1.0.1 | v2.0.0 | v2.1.0 | v2.2.0 |
|-------|--------|--------|--------|--------|
| Context rot killing run | YES | PARTIAL | MITIGATED | MITIGATED |
| Reviewer artifact corruption | YES | YES | RESOLVED | RESOLVED |
| Coder false-positive loops | YES | YES (4+) | RESOLVED (0) | PARTIAL (degraded) |
| Architect wrong model | — | YES | YES | RESOLVED (Sonnet 4.6) |
| No curl/smoke before Reviewer | — | YES | YES | RESOLVED |
| Coder handoff format issues | — | YES | PARTIAL | PARTIAL (1 rejection) |
| Red card not triggered properly | — | YES | YES | RESOLVED |
| Plan too verbose | — | PARTIAL | YES (~380 lines) | IMPROVED (cap added) |
| Code quality: naming chaos | — | — | — | NEW |
| Code quality: model proliferation | — | — | — | NEW |
| Coder terminal mega-commands | — | YES | YES | YES (recurring) |
| Reviewer adds no code quality value | — | — | PARTIAL | YES |

---

## Codebase Refactoring Plan

**Goal:** Establish a clean, documented package structure and naming convention that the Architect and Coder must follow for all future features. Fix the most impactful code quality issues identified across v2.1.0 and v2.2.0 runs.

**Principle:** This refactoring does not add features. It restructures and consolidates existing code to create a maintainable foundation.

---

### R1 — Canonical Package Structure

Define and document the following layout in `code-guidance.md`:

```
com.gitlabflow.floworchestrator/
├── FlowOrchestratorApplication.java
├── common/
│   ├── errors/              # ErrorCode, IntegrationException, ValidationException
│   └── web/                 # GlobalExceptionHandler, ErrorResponse
├── config/                  # @Configuration, @ConfigurationProperties
│   ├── GitLabProperties.java
│   ├── IssuesApiProperties.java
│   └── GitLabRestClientConfig.java
├── domain/                  # Pure business types (no framework deps)
│   └── (future: business rules, aggregates, domain services)
├── orchestration/
│   └── issues/
│       ├── model/           # Orchestration-layer models (Issue, IssuePage, IssueQuery, etc.)
│       ├── port/            # Provider-agnostic interfaces (IssuesPort)
│       ├── service/         # Use-case services (IssuesService)
│       └── api/
│           ├── request/     # Inbound API request records
│           ├── response/    # Outbound API response records
│           ├── mapper/      # API ↔ orchestration mappers
│           └── IssuesController.java
└── integration/
    └── gitlab/
        ├── GitLabProjectLocator.java
        ├── GitLabExceptionMapper.java
        └── issues/
            ├── model/       # GitLab-specific request/response records
            ├── mapper/      # GitLab ↔ orchestration mappers
            └── GitLabIssuesAdapter.java  # implements IssuesPort
```

**Key rules for `code-guidance.md`:**
- One sub-package per business capability (e.g., `issues`, `projects`, `pipelines`)
- Within each capability: `model/`, `port/`, `service/`, `api/` at orchestration level
- Within each capability at integration level: `model/`, `mapper/`, one adapter class
- Singular feature names for commands/queries; plural for collections (`Issue` not `Issues` for the model)
- Ports named `<Capability>Port` (e.g., `IssuesPort`), not split per operation

---

### R2 — Consolidate Ports Into Single Interface Per Capability

**Current:** `GetIssuesPort` + `CreateIssuePort` (2 interfaces, 2 adapters)
**Target:** `IssuesPort` (1 interface with `getIssues()` and `createIssue()`)

**Changes:**
- Merge `GetIssuesPort` and `CreateIssuePort` → `IssuesPort`
- Merge `GitLabIssuesAdapter` and `GitLabCreateIssueAdapter` → `GitLabIssuesAdapter`
- Merge `GetIssuesService` and `CreateIssueService` → `IssuesService`
- Remove `@ConditionalOnBean` from CreateIssueService (fold into single service)
- Update IssuesController to inject 1 service instead of 2

**Impact:** -4 files, -1 constructor dependency in controller, consistent naming.

---

### R3 — Consolidate Mappers

**Current (orchestration/api):** 4 mapper classes: `IssuesRequestMapper`, `IssuesResponseMapper`, `CreateIssueRequestMapper`, `CreateIssueResponseMapper`
**Target:** 2 mapper classes: `IssuesApiMapper` (handles all issues API mapping), or keep request/response split as `IssuesRequestMapper` + `IssuesResponseMapper` with expanded methods.

**Current (integration/gitlab):** 2 mapper classes: `GitLabIssueMapper`, `GitLabCreateIssueMapper`
**Target:** 1 mapper class: `GitLabIssuesMapper`

**Changes:**
- Merge `CreateIssueRequestMapper` → `IssuesRequestMapper.toCommand(CreateIssueRequest)`
- Merge `CreateIssueResponseMapper` → `IssuesResponseMapper.toCreateResponse(CreatedIssue)`
- Merge `GitLabCreateIssueMapper` into `GitLabIssuesMapper`
- Update all injection points

**Impact:** -3 files, fewer beans, simpler wiring.

---

### R4 — Eliminate Mirror DTOs Where Possible

**Current:** `IssueResponseItem` is identical to `Issue`. `CreateIssueResponse` is identical to `CreatedIssue`.

**Decision:** Keep the separation but make it intentional. API response records exist because:
1. API responses may diverge from domain models (e.g., adding `_links`, `createdAt`)
2. Jackson annotations belong on API records, not domain models

**Action:** Add a comment in `code-guidance.md`: "API response records may mirror orchestration models initially. Do not merge them — they serve different evolution paths. Do not create a new response record if it would be identical and you have no serialization or presentation divergence planned."

This is not a code change — it is a decision record that prevents future Architects from creating more mirrors *and* prevents future refactorers from removing the separation too eagerly.

---

### R5 — Fix Duplicate Exception Handling Pattern

**Current:** Both adapters have identical `try/catch` structure:
```java
try { ... }
catch (RestClientResponseException e) { throw mapper.fromHttpFailure(e, source); }
catch (RestClientException e) { throw mapper.fromTransportFailure(source); }
catch (RuntimeException e) { throw mapper.fromTransportFailure(source); }
```

The `RestClientException` and `RuntimeException` catches both call `fromTransportFailure` — this is the exact pattern flagged in run notes.

**Fix:** `RestClientException extends RuntimeException`, so the `RuntimeException` catch is only hit for non-RestClient exceptions. Collapse to:

```java
try { ... }
catch (RestClientResponseException e) { throw mapper.fromHttpFailure(e, source); }
catch (RuntimeException e) { throw mapper.fromTransportFailure(source); }
```

After consolidating ports (R2), this pattern exists in only one adapter class.

**Impact:** Cleaner error handling, removes the "what's the point?" smell.

---

### R6 — Fix `CreateIssueCommand` Defensive Constructor

**Current:**
```java
public CreateIssueCommand {
    labels = labels == null ? List.of() : List.copyOf(labels);
}
```

**Issue:** Mixes null-coalescing with immutable copy. The ternary pattern was flagged in run notes.

**Fix:**
```java
public CreateIssueCommand {
    labels = List.copyOf(Objects.requireNonNullElse(labels, List.of()));
}
```

Or, if the intent is that null labels should stay empty:
```java
public CreateIssueCommand {
    labels = labels == null ? List.of() : List.copyOf(labels);
}
```

Actually the current code *is* functionally correct. The aesthetic issue is the pattern being used inconsistently across records. **Standardize** the pattern in `code-guidance.md`:

> For record compact constructors that defensively copy collections, use:
> `field = field == null ? List.of() : List.copyOf(field);`
> This is the canonical pattern. Do not use `Objects.requireNonNullElse` for this case — it adds a method call without improving readability.

---

### R7 — Rename `CreateIssueCommand` → `CreateIssueInput`

**Current:** `Command` implies CQRS which does not exist in this project.
**Target:** `CreateIssueInput` — neutral, descriptive, no pattern implication.

**Changes:**
- Rename `CreateIssueCommand` → `CreateIssueInput` in `orchestration/issues/model/`
- Update all references (service, port, mappers, tests)

**Impact:** Removes misleading naming, aligns with the project's actual architecture.

---

### R8 — Standardize Naming Conventions

Add to `code-guidance.md`:

| Component Type | Naming Pattern | Example |
|---------------|---------------|---------|
| Port interface | `<Capability>Port` | `IssuesPort` |
| Adapter | `GitLab<Capability>Adapter` | `GitLabIssuesAdapter` |
| Service | `<Capability>Service` | `IssuesService` |
| Orchestration model | Singular noun | `Issue`, `IssueQuery`, `IssuePage` |
| API request record | `<Action><Capability>Request` | `CreateIssueRequest`, `SearchIssuesRequest` |
| API response record | `<Action><Capability>Response` or `<Capability>Response` | `CreateIssueResponse`, `IssuesResponse` |
| API mapper | `<Capability>RequestMapper`, `<Capability>ResponseMapper` | `IssuesRequestMapper` |
| Integration mapper | `GitLab<Capability>Mapper` | `GitLabIssuesMapper` |
| Integration model | `GitLab<Noun>` | `GitLabIssueResponse`, `GitLabCreateIssueRequest` |
| Controller | `<Capability>Controller` | `IssuesController` |

---

### R9 — Add Java Formatting to Build

**Action:** Add `fmt-maven-plugin` (Google Java Format) to `pom.xml`:

```xml
<plugin>
    <groupId>com.spotify.fmt</groupId>
    <artifactId>fmt-maven-plugin</artifactId>
    <version>2.25</version>
    <executions>
        <execution>
            <goals><goal>check</goal></goals>
        </execution>
    </executions>
</plugin>
```

Run `mvn fmt:format` once to baseline, then `check` goal fails the build on unformatted code.

**Impact:** Eliminates all indentation/formatting issues permanently.

---

### R10 — Resolve Health Endpoint

**Decision:** Add `spring-boot-starter-actuator` to `pom.xml`. It is a 2-line change that gives `/actuator/health` for free. The smoke-test script already checks it.

```xml
<dependency>
    <groupId>org.springframework.boot</groupId>
    <artifactId>spring-boot-starter-actuator</artifactId>
</dependency>
```

**Impact:** Removes recurring smoke-test failure.

---

### Refactoring Execution Order

| Phase | Refactoring | Risk | Effort |
|-------|------------|------|--------|
| 1 | R8 — Add naming conventions to `code-guidance.md` | None (doc only) | 15 min |
| 1 | R1 — Add canonical package structure to `code-guidance.md` | None (doc only) | 15 min |
| 1 | R4 — Add mirror DTO policy to `code-guidance.md` | None (doc only) | 5 min |
| 1 | R6 — Add collection defense pattern to `code-guidance.md` | None (doc only) | 5 min |
| 2 | R10 — Add actuator dependency | Minimal | 5 min |
| 2 | R9 — Add formatting plugin + baseline format | Low | 20 min |
| 3 | R2 — Consolidate ports + adapters + services | Medium (test updates) | 1-2h |
| 3 | R5 — Fix duplicate exception handling | Low (part of R2) | 15 min |
| 3 | R3 — Consolidate mappers | Medium (test updates) | 1h |
| 3 | R7 — Rename CreateIssueCommand → CreateIssueInput | Low | 20 min |
| 4 | Move files to canonical package structure (R1 applied to code) | High (many file moves) | 2h |

**Phase 1** is documentation-only and immediately improves future runs.
**Phase 2** is low-risk build config.
**Phase 3** is code consolidation — run full quality gate after each change.
**Phase 4** is the big move — best done as a single batch with full verification.
